domain: Hmong paj ntaub pictorial story cloth (Hmong women's narrative embroidery; sequential pictorial panels with geometric border framing; refugee-camp origin Thailand 1970s)
specific_mechanism: Long-form generation organized as K panel segments, each bracketed by an opening frame F_i^open and closing frame F_i^close. F_i^open encodes (panel-id, narrative-position, topic-anchor). F_i^close echoes the topic-anchor in a normalized form used as the next panel's input handoff signal. Panel boundaries are explicit tokens with cross-attention restricted to within-panel + previous-panel frame echo.
llm_application: PAJ-NTAUB-PANEL: panel-bordered narrative cascade for long-form generation. Generation proceeds as K panel segments P_1..P_K, each preceded by a frame-border token F_i^open = [PANEL_i_START, pos=i/K, topic_anchor=Θ_i] and followed by F_i^close = [PANEL_i_END, anchor_echo=Θ_i_verified]. Cross-segment context propagation uses only F_{i-1}^close → F_i^open handoff (compressed); within-panel attention is full but cross-panel attention is restricted to consecutive frame echo signals. Differs from Chain-of-Agents NeurIPS 2024 (segment-wise summary handoff without explicit frame-border verification token), NEXUSSUM ACL 2025 (hierarchical agent decomposition without panel-border framing), Narrative Weaver 2603.06688 (cross-panel consistency in visual modality, not text frame-border tokens). Differs from R422 CHASQUI-RELAY (quipu summary handoff between memoryless agents) by explicit frame-border bracketing token structure within single model.
novelty_claim: Chain-of-Agents + NEXUSSUM + Narrative Weaver + Story-generation survey cover serialized narrative cascade space. PAJ-NTAUB-PANEL adds (a) panel-id+position frame-border tokens + (b) topic-anchor verification echo + (c) cross-panel attention restricted to consecutive frame-echo signal. Recombination.
